description:
You are a security engineer who has just finished an emergency credential-rotation exercise.  
    A raw log of the session is already present on the machine:

        /home/user/security/logs/2024-credential-rotation.log

    Your job is to extract the key facts an auditor will later review.  
    The auditor wants three artefacts, all of which **must be produced with the exact names,
    directory structure and formatting rules that follow** (an automated test will
    check them byte-for-byte, including line-endings and capitalisation).

    1.  /home/user/security/analysis/critical_rotation_events.log  
        • Contains every log line that simultaneously meets BOTH of these conditions:  
          a) The log-level field (the text enclosed in square brackets: “[WARNING]”, “[ERROR]”, etc.) is either WARNING or ERROR.  
          b) The message text includes the exact, case-sensitive phrase “credential expired”.  
        • Preserve the original chronological order of the selected lines.  
        • Do not include any extra characters, blank lines or headers: the file must be a pure,
          line-for-line subset of the original log.

    2.  /home/user/security/analysis/svc_account_hits.log  
        • Contains every log line where the message refers to a user whose name starts with “svc_”.  
          (The format in the raw log is:  User 'svc_name' …).  
        • Again, keep the lines in their original order and do not add headers, comments or blank lines.

    3.  /home/user/security/analysis/rotation_summary.txt  
        • A three-line ASCII text file (Unix line-endings).  
        • Exactly this layout, including the single spaces and the colon punctuation:  
              Total events analyzed: <integer>  
              Critical rotation events: <integer>  
              Unique services marked for rotation: <integer>  
        • Meanings:  
          – “Total events analyzed” is just the number of lines in the source log.  
          – “Critical rotation events” is the number of lines you placed in critical_rotation_events.log.  
          – “Unique services marked for rotation” is the count of distinct Service names
            that appear inside those critical lines (the service name is the string found
            between the single quotes after the word “Service” in each log entry).

    Additional rules & tips
    -----------------------
    • Create the directory /home/user/security/analysis/ if it does not already exist; all
      files must end up exactly there.  
    • Use any standard command-line tools and regular expressions available in a typical
      Linux user shell (grep, awk, sed, sort, uniq, wc …) — but you must decide the
      pipeline yourself.  
    • The automated tests are strict: no trailing spaces, no extra blank lines, correct
      capitalisation, correct file paths.  
    • Do everything from the terminal; do not modify the original log file.

    When you are done, the auditor should find the three files in the analysis
    directory with the precise content and formatting described above.
truth:
Pre-existing artefacts before the agent starts
    ---------------------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/security/logs
    2. File: /home/user/security/logs/2024-credential-rotation.log  
       Exact 20-line content (UTF-8, LF line-endings):

        2024-06-11T14:22:01Z [INFO] Service 'api-gateway' credential rotation successful
        2024-06-11T14:25:47Z [WARNING] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, rotation required
        2024-06-11T14:26:02Z [ERROR] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, failed automatic rotation
        2024-06-11T14:30:12Z [INFO] User 'alice' authenticated to 'git-server'
        2024-06-11T14:35:55Z [INFO] Service 'db-backend' credential rotation scheduled
        2024-06-11T14:36:07Z [ERROR] Service 'db-backend' credential expired, rotation halted
        2024-06-11T14:40:11Z [WARNING] Service 'billing-service' credential expired, awaiting rotation
        2024-06-11T14:45:23Z [INFO] Service 'message-queue' credential rotation successful
        2024-06-11T14:47:58Z [ERROR] Service 'message-queue' credential expired, failed automatic rotation
        2024-06-11T14:48:12Z [INFO] User 'svc_analytics' obtained temporary token
        2024-06-11T14:49:42Z [INFO] User 'svc_billing' token refresh completed
        2024-06-11T14:50:21Z [WARNING] User 'svc_marketing' failed token refresh attempt
        2024-06-11T14:52:07Z [INFO] Background job 'rotate-keys' finished
        2024-06-11T14:53:17Z [INFO] Service 'search-service' credential rotation successful
        2024-06-11T14:54:20Z [WARNING] Service 'search-service' credential expired, rotation required
        2024-06-11T14:55:09Z [INFO] User 'bob' logged out
        2024-06-11T14:56:30Z [INFO] User 'svc_billing' credential renewed
        2024-06-11T14:57:41Z [ERROR] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, rotation attempt failed again
        2024-06-11T15:01:26Z [INFO] CRON 'cleanup-temp' completed
        2024-06-11T15:02:57Z [INFO] User 'svc_reporting' authenticated

    Expected artefacts after successful completion
    ----------------------------------------------
    1. Directory: /home/user/security/analysis  (permissions: 755 or default user umask)

    2. File: /home/user/security/analysis/critical_rotation_events.log  
       Contents (exact, 7 lines):

        2024-06-11T14:25:47Z [WARNING] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, rotation required
        2024-06-11T14:26:02Z [ERROR] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, failed automatic rotation
        2024-06-11T14:36:07Z [ERROR] Service 'db-backend' credential expired, rotation halted
        2024-06-11T14:40:11Z [WARNING] Service 'billing-service' credential expired, awaiting rotation
        2024-06-11T14:47:58Z [ERROR] Service 'message-queue' credential expired, failed automatic rotation
        2024-06-11T14:54:20Z [WARNING] Service 'search-service' credential expired, rotation required
        2024-06-11T14:57:41Z [ERROR] Service 'auth-service' credential expired, rotation attempt failed again

    3. File: /home/user/security/analysis/svc_account_hits.log  
       Contents (exact, 5 lines):

        2024-06-11T14:48:12Z [INFO] User 'svc_analytics' obtained temporary token
        2024-06-11T14:49:42Z [INFO] User 'svc_billing' token refresh completed
        2024-06-11T14:50:21Z [WARNING] User 'svc_marketing' failed token refresh attempt
        2024-06-11T14:56:30Z [INFO] User 'svc_billing' credential renewed
        2024-06-11T15:02:57Z [INFO] User 'svc_reporting' authenticated

    4. File: /home/user/security/analysis/rotation_summary.txt  
       Contents (exact, 3 lines):

        Total events analyzed: 20
        Critical rotation events: 7
        Unique services marked for rotation: 5
